layout: tool-doc
title: "phrasplit Command Line Interface"
permalink: /tools/phrasplit/cli/
nav_tool: phrasplit
---

<!-- GENERATED by sphinxpress. Do not edit by hand. -->

<section id="command-line-interface">
<h1>Command Line Interface</h1>
<p>phrasplit provides a command-line interface for processing text files.</p>
<p>By default, the CLI automatically detects if spaCy is installed and uses it for best
accuracy. You can use the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">--simple</span></code> flag to force fast regex-based splitting that
doesn’t require spaCy.</p>
<section id="basic-usage">
<h2>Basic Usage</h2>
<p>The CLI has four main commands:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sentences</span></code> - Split text into sentences</p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">clauses</span></code> - Split text into clauses (at commas)</p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">paragraphs</span></code> - Split text into paragraphs</p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">longlines</span></code> - Split long lines at natural boundaries</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="getting-help">
<h2>Getting Help</h2>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># Show available commands</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>--help

<span class="c1"># Show help for a specific command</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences<span class="w"> </span>--help
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="splitting-sentences">
<h2>Splitting Sentences</h2>
<p>Split a text file into sentences:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># Auto-detect mode (uses spaCy if available)</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences<span class="w"> </span>input.txt

<span class="c1"># Output to file</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences<span class="w"> </span>input.txt<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>output.txt

<span class="c1"># Force simple mode (60x faster, no spaCy required)</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences<span class="w"> </span>input.txt<span class="w"> </span>--simple

<span class="c1"># Use a different spaCy model (only when using spaCy mode)</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences<span class="w"> </span>input.txt<span class="w"> </span>--model<span class="w"> </span>en_core_web_lg
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="splitting-clauses">
<h2>Splitting Clauses</h2>
<p>Split text into comma-separated parts:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>clauses<span class="w"> </span>input.txt
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>clauses<span class="w"> </span>input.txt<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>output.txt

<span class="c1"># Use simple mode for faster processing</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>clauses<span class="w"> </span>input.txt<span class="w"> </span>--simple
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="splitting-paragraphs">
<h2>Splitting Paragraphs</h2>
<p>Split text into paragraphs (no spaCy needed):</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>paragraphs<span class="w"> </span>input.txt
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>paragraphs<span class="w"> </span>input.txt<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>output.txt
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="splitting-long-lines">
<h2>Splitting Long Lines</h2>
<p>Split long lines to fit within a maximum length:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># Default max length is 80 characters</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>longlines<span class="w"> </span>input.txt

<span class="c1"># Custom max length</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>longlines<span class="w"> </span>input.txt<span class="w"> </span>--max-length<span class="w"> </span><span class="m">60</span>

<span class="c1"># Use simple mode for faster processing</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>longlines<span class="w"> </span>input.txt<span class="w"> </span>--simple<span class="w"> </span>--max-length<span class="w"> </span><span class="m">60</span>

<span class="c1"># Output to file with custom length</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>longlines<span class="w"> </span>input.txt<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>output.txt<span class="w"> </span>-l<span class="w"> </span><span class="m">100</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="reading-from-stdin">
<h2>Reading from stdin</h2>
<p>All commands support reading from stdin by omitting the input file or using <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">-</span></code>:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># Pipe input</span>
<span class="nb">echo</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Hello world. This is a test.&quot;</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">|</span><span class="w"> </span>phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences

<span class="c1"># Redirect input</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences<span class="w"> </span>&lt;<span class="w"> </span>input.txt

<span class="c1"># Explicit stdin with dash</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences<span class="w"> </span>-<span class="w"> </span>&lt;<span class="w"> </span>input.txt

<span class="c1"># Combine with output file</span>
cat<span class="w"> </span>input.txt<span class="w"> </span><span class="p">|</span><span class="w"> </span>phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>clauses<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>output.txt
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="command-options">
<h2>Command Options</h2>
<section id="sentences">
<h3>sentences</h3>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>Usage: phrasplit sentences [OPTIONS] [INPUT_FILE]

Split text into sentences.

By default, uses spaCy if available for best accuracy. Use --simple for
faster regex-based splitting that doesn&#39;t require spaCy.

Options:
  -o, --output PATH   Output file (default: stdout)
  -m, --model TEXT    spaCy language model (default: en_core_web_sm)
  --simple            Use simple regex-based splitting (faster, no spaCy required)
  --help              Show this message and exit.
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="clauses">
<h3>clauses</h3>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>Usage: phrasplit clauses [OPTIONS] [INPUT_FILE]

Split text into clauses (at commas).

By default, uses spaCy if available for best accuracy. Use --simple for
faster regex-based splitting that doesn&#39;t require spaCy.

Options:
  -o, --output PATH   Output file (default: stdout)
  -m, --model TEXT    spaCy language model (default: en_core_web_sm)
  --simple            Use simple regex-based splitting (faster, no spaCy required)
  --help              Show this message and exit.
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="paragraphs">
<h3>paragraphs</h3>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>Usage: phrasplit paragraphs [OPTIONS] [INPUT_FILE]

Split text into paragraphs.

Options:
  -o, --output PATH   Output file (default: stdout)
  --help              Show this message and exit.
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="longlines">
<h3>longlines</h3>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>Usage: phrasplit longlines [OPTIONS] [INPUT_FILE]

Split long lines at sentence/clause boundaries.

By default, uses spaCy if available for best accuracy. Use --simple for
faster regex-based splitting that doesn&#39;t require spaCy.

Options:
  -o, --output PATH        Output file (default: stdout)
  -l, --max-length INTEGER Maximum line length (default: 80, must be &gt;= 1)
  -m, --model TEXT         spaCy language model (default: en_core_web_sm)
  --simple                 Use simple regex-based splitting (faster, no spaCy required)
  --help                   Show this message and exit.
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
</section>
<section id="examples">
<h2>Examples</h2>
<p>Process a book for audiobook creation:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># Split into sentences first (using simple mode for speed)</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences<span class="w"> </span>book.txt<span class="w"> </span>--simple<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>book_sentences.txt

<span class="c1"># Then split long sentences into clauses</span>
phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>clauses<span class="w"> </span>book_sentences.txt<span class="w"> </span>--simple<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>book_clauses.txt
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Create subtitles with line length limits:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>longlines<span class="w"> </span>transcript.txt<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>subtitles.txt<span class="w"> </span>--max-length<span class="w"> </span><span class="m">42</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Speed comparison example:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="c1"># Using spaCy mode (higher accuracy, slower)</span>
<span class="nb">time</span><span class="w"> </span>phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences<span class="w"> </span>large_book.txt<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>output_spacy.txt

<span class="c1"># Using simple mode (60x faster, good for well-formatted text)</span>
<span class="nb">time</span><span class="w"> </span>phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences<span class="w"> </span>large_book.txt<span class="w"> </span>--simple<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>output_simple.txt
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Pipeline example with multiple tools:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>cat<span class="w"> </span>book.txt<span class="w"> </span><span class="p">|</span><span class="w"> </span>phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>sentences<span class="w"> </span>--simple<span class="w"> </span><span class="p">|</span><span class="w"> </span>phrasplit<span class="w"> </span>clauses<span class="w"> </span>--simple<span class="w"> </span>&gt;<span class="w"> </span>output.txt
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
</section>
